title: Rich Velvety French Vegetable Ratatouille
description: A winey and velvety French Normandy dish featuring vegetables in a rich sauce, served in a tajine clay vessel, with anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients and a shio-koji cure, although it does not actually include hard taco shells as that would not be traditional or realistic for this type of dish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the eggplant, zucchini, and bell peppers with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the vegetables in a single layer on a large baking sheet and roast in the oven for 30 minutes.
In a large skillet, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until the onion is translucent.
Add the red wine, shio-koji, tomato paste, and thyme to the skillet and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the roasted vegetables to the skillet and stir to combine with the sauce.
Transfer the ratatouille to a tajine clay vessel and bake in the oven for an additional 20 minutes.
Remove the ratatouille from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the ratatouille hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
